label: Streetwear Bold
description: High-contrast storefronts with strong product attitude and campaign energy.
signatureMotif: hard contrast, oversized type, and campaign-led product storytelling
promptHints: Favor campaign attitude and drop culture over safe retail neutrality.
Product cards and promotional bands should feel fast, bold, and scarce.

layouts: Use a full-bleed dark hero with oversized condensed headline, one dominant sneaker image, and a stacked drop countdown beside the CTA.
Build collection pages as a dense 3-column product wall with promotional tiles injected between products like campaign posters.
Use a product detail layout with a large left image stage, right-side size selector block, and a slim scarcity strip above purchase controls.
Design the homepage with horizontal campaign bands separating sections, alternating featured product spotlights and new-drop grids.
Use a raised carousel or slider for limited releases, with cropped product shots, bold labels, and quick-buy actions pinned below.
motifs: Use hard black-charcoal surfaces with stark white type, hot orange CTAs, and occasional acid-green accents for release energy.
Pair oversized all-caps condensed headlines with tight tracking, compact utility text, and bold numeric callouts for drops and pricing.
Make cards feel sharp and fast with visible borders, medium radius corners, and poster-like image crops that intentionally feel aggressive.
Add scarcity cues at first glance through countdown pills, low-stock tags, and thin promotional bars spanning the viewport.
antiPatterns: Avoid soft pastel palettes, airy lifestyle minimalism, or gentle wellness styling that dilutes the storefront’s streetwear attitude.
Never use polite boutique product grids with excessive whitespace; this variant should feel packed, urgent, and campaign-driven.
Avoid glossy luxury editorials with serif typography and muted elegance; the voice here should feel raw, loud, and immediate.
Do not rely on neutral retail UI alone without promo bands, scarcity messaging, or drop framing around featured products.
